tagline: You don't need to track every coffee. Just split your paycheck smartly once a month.
over_by: Over by {pct}% — remove some to reach 100%
empty_state: Enter your salary and set up categories to see the split
budgeting_approaches_subtitle: Two widely used frameworks for splitting your income. Pick the one that fits your style, or mix ideas from both.
guidelines_disclaimer: These are general guidelines, not financial advice. Your ideal split depends on income level, cost of living, debt obligations, and personal goals.
six_accounts_description: Popularised by T. Harv Eker in
six_accounts_disclaimer: The percentages above total 100% of after-tax income. Adapt the ratios to your reality — the key principle is giving every dollar a purpose.
housing_note: Includes rent or mortgage. Keeping this under 30% avoids being "house poor."
savings_note: Pay yourself first — the 50/30/20 rule suggests at least 20% toward savings and debt repayment.
food_note: Cooking at home keeps this on the lower end; dining out pushes it higher.
transport_note: Gas, insurance, public transit, or car payment. Aim for the lower range if possible.
utilities_note: Electricity, water, internet, phone. Relatively fixed — budget a consistent amount.
entertainment_note: Part of the "wants" bucket. Guilt-free spending once essentials are covered.
emergency_note: Not a monthly %, but a target reserve. Build it gradually, then maintain it.
necessities_note: Rent, food, bills, transport — everything you need to live day-to-day.
financial_freedom_note: Investments and passive-income building. Never spend this — let it compound.
long_term_savings_note: Big future purchases: house deposit, car, holidays. Goal-based saving.
education_note: Courses, books, coaching — invest in growing your earning potential.
play_note: Guilt-free fun money. Spend it every month to reward yourself.
give_note: Charity, gifts, helping others. Builds gratitude and community.
note_tooltip: See what belongs to this category
category_disabled: Disabled — won't count toward your budget
delete_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
fixed_tooltip: Fixed amount — won’t change when salary changes
alert_all_good: Looking good! Your spending is within recommended guidelines. Keep it up!
alert_housing_over: You're spending {pct}% ({amount}) on housing — try to keep it within {recommended} ({recommendedAmount}). This prevents being "house poor" and frees cash for savings.
alert_savings_under: You're only saving {pct}% ({amount}) — aim for at least {recommended} ({recommendedAmount}). The 50/30/20 rule suggests at least 20% toward savings and debt repayment.
alert_food_over: Food is at {pct}% ({amount}) — try to keep it within {recommended} ({recommendedAmount}). Cooking at home more often can help bring this down.
alert_transport_over: Transport at {pct}% ({amount}) exceeds the recommended {recommended} ({recommendedAmount}). Consider carpooling, public transit, or optimizing routes.
alert_utilities_over: Utilities at {pct}% ({amount}) are above the typical {recommended} range ({recommendedAmount}). Review subscriptions and energy usage for potential savings.
alert_entertainment_over: Entertainment is at {pct}% ({amount}) — aim for {recommended} ({recommendedAmount}). Make sure essentials and savings are covered first.
alert_disclaimer: Based on the 50/30/20 rule. These are general guidelines, not financial advice — your ideal split depends on your personal situation.
